Fix search: load graph sidecar on startup, add dimension logging (#14)

The graph sidecar was created empty and never loaded from PG, causing
graph and structural dimensions to always return zero results. Now
calls full_reload() during AppState initialization.

Also adds debug-level logging for per-dimension result counts and
query embedding status to aid future search diagnostics.

Add per-lot trailing stop mode: independent trail params per lot

Backend (risk_manager.py):
- New LotTrailingState class tracks per-lot HWM, trail price, activation, tranches
- trailing_stop.mode config: "uniform" (default, no change) or "per_lot"

  entry price, HWM, and optional trail_pct/activation_pct overrides
- Per-lot fills route to correct lot state, tranche advancement per-lot
- Mode switching via hot-modify: uniform->per_lot seeds from aggregate HWM,
  per_lot->uniform collapses to max HWM

- Backward compatible: absent mode defaults to "uniform"

Frontend (SignalsTab.tsx):

- Per-lot trail status badges show trail price when active
- grpHasTrail check extended for trailing_lot_* level keys

commit.message:
Remove WAF, add Lambda concurrency kill switch with configurable re-enable

- Remove CDK-managed WAF (security.ts deleted); replace with CloudFront
  flat-rate plan (enrolled in AWS console, $0/month Free tier)
- Kill switch now sets reservedConcurrentExecutions=0 on all Lambdas;
  API Gateway returns 429 with no invocations
- EventBridge Scheduler auto-restores concurrency after configurable delay:
  beta=3 min (for testing), prod=4 hours
- Kill switch deployed in beta (manual SNS trigger) and prod (CloudWatch
  SustainedTrafficAlarm: API GW ≥550 req/min × 3 consecutive windows)
- Beta gets standalone SNS topic with KillSwitchTopicArn stack output;
  prod reuses monitoring alertTopic
- Re-enable handler calls DeleteFunctionConcurrency for beta (no reserved
  concurrency normally) and PutFunctionConcurrency for prod
- API Gateway throttle (burst/rate) now configurable via ThrottleConfig in
  shared environment config (all envs: rate=10 req/s, burst=20)
- Add killSwitchEnabled feature flag and killSwitchReEnableMinutes to
  MonitoringConfig in shared types
- Add BOTPROTECTION.md: 5 defense layers, CloudFront flat-rate plan setup,
  kill switch trigger/recovery per env, worst-case cost analysis (~$91/month)
- Update all docs (COSTS, DEPLOYMENT, SPECIFICATION, README) to remove
  WAF/Cloudflare references and cross-reference BOTPROTECTION.md
- Rewrite kill-switch-handler tests for Lambda/Scheduler SDK; delete
  security.test.ts (construct removed); 313 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-resolve flat key-value runtime inputs for pipeline execution (#62)

* feat: auto-resolve flat key-value runtime inputs for pipeline execution

Users can now pass simple key-value pairs like {branch: "main", env: "prod"}
when executing pipelines instead of constructing the full nested runtime input
YAML. The system auto-fetches the pipeline's input template, matches user
values to <+input> placeholders by variable name, and produces the correct
YAML for the execute API.

Changes:
- Add runtime-input-resolver utility (template fetch, YAML substitution)
- Add runtime_input_template resource type for discovering pipeline inputs
- Add input_set_ids query param support on pipeline run action
- Pre-process flat inputs in harness_execute before dispatch
- 22 new tests covering resolver, substitution, and integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: approval comments not reaching Harness API when passed via body

The approve/reject bodyBuilders only checked `input.comments` (set via
params), but LLMs often pass comments via `body: {comments: "..."}` which
lands at `input.body.comments`. Now both bodyBuilders check input.body
as a fallback so comments work regardless of how the LLM structures the call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Rebuild as visual audit mode

Instead of following step-by-step instructions, the system now:
1. Identifies which Klaviyo pages each article references
2. Screenshots those pages in the live app
3. Asks GPT to compare article text against live screenshots
4. Reports UI mismatches (renamed buttons, moved menus, etc.)

Much faster, no account state needed, no fragile CSS selectors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: MergeRowGroups multi-segment ordering and DropDuplicatedRows (#483)

## Summary

Fixes #471 — two bugs in `MergeRowGroups`:

- **Multi-segment merge produced unsorted output.** When
`MergeRowGroups` had both non-overlapping and overlapping segments
(e.g., groups `[1,2,3]`, `[4,6,8]`, `[5,7,9]`), it wrapped them in a
`multiRowGroup` whose `Rows()` read column pages directly — bypassing
the heap merge in `mergedRowGroup.Rows()`. Added `sortedSegmentRowGroup`
that overrides `Rows()` to concatenate each segment's own reader,
preserving merge ordering within `mergedRowGroup` segments.
- **`DropDuplicatedRows` was ignored.** The flag was accepted via
`RowGroupConfig.Sorting` but never applied by `MergeRowGroups`. Now
`mergedRowGroup`, `sortedSegmentRowGroup`, and a new `dedupRowGroup` all
wrap their readers with `DedupeRowReader` when the flag is set.

## Test plan

- [x] `TestMergeRowGroupsOverlappingSegments` — 3 groups with mixed
overlapping/non-overlapping segments, verifies correct sorted order
- [x] `TestMergeRowGroupsDropDuplicatedRows` — 3 subtests covering
overlapping groups with dupes, multi-segment with dupes, and single
group with dupes
- [x] All existing tests pass (`go test ./...`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Add ref-specific builds for panels and workers

Extend getBuild(unitPath, ref?) to build at any git ref (branch, tag, or
commit SHA). Content-addressed build store provides automatic caching —
same ref content = same build key = cache hit.

- Build system: ref path resolves commit, walks dep tree for commitMap,
  recomputes EV via recomputeFromNode, falls through to buildUnit
- HTTP layer: ?ref= query param on panel URLs, composite flight/error
  keys isolate ref from HEAD builds, servingCache stays source-keyed
- Workers: ref replaces pin (commit SHA = immutable pinning), ref-targeted
  workers skip auto-restart on HEAD push
- Remove dead createBuildServiceHandler, export resolveDepRefToGitRef

- audio.py: Podcastfy + Edge TTS wrapper (NotebookLM placeholder for future)
- publisher.py: Self-hosted RSS XML generation + GitHub Releases for MP3 hosting
- pipeline.py: Daily/weekly orchestrator with threshold logic (>=3 CGT articles)
- GitHub Actions: cron workflows for daily Mon-Fri + weekly Friday digest
- 28 tests passing, ruff clean

feat: add GitLab provider, MCP server, tests, and agent skills

GitLab Provider:
- Full Provider interface implementation (releases, tags, latest)
- URL-encoded project paths for GitLab API
- PRIVATE-TOKEN auth header support
- Complete test suite with httptest mock server

MCP Server (5 tools):
- list_releases: list releases for any GitHub/GitLab repo
- get_latest_release: get latest release with notes
- list_tags: list git tags with commit SHAs
- check_services: check all configured services
- find_outdated: find services behind latest release
- HTTP+SSE transport on configurable port (default 7891)
- Graceful shutdown with signal handling

Testing:
- 17 tests across GitHub + GitLab providers
- Table-driven tests with httptest mock servers
- Content validation and error handling tests

Skills:
- Installed 9 agent skills from skills.sh

Update specs to reflect Waves 1-4 implementation

05-ingestion: format converters (Stage 1.5), overlapping chunking
config, parallel extraction, GLiNER2 sidecar, 4-tier entity
resolution, rel-type resolution, OpenAI dimension parameter, node
embeddings (Stage 7.5), doc-level chunk removal.

06-search: graph dimension auto-seed detection, lexical ts_headline
snippet generation.

03-storage: embedding dimensions 2048/256, migration 003 source
embedding, sources table schema update.

01-architecture: evaluation crate layer.

11-evaluation: current fixture-based state vs aspirational RAGAS.

docs: Великий Календар design document v3

Unified weekly calendar model — single table for all grid blocks
(plan slots + external Outlook/Teams/Viva events). Key decisions:
- weekly_calendar_entries replaces weekly_plan_slots
- Delta query + webhook for Graph sync (PULL→WORK→PUSH pattern)
- daily_task_id on entries for many-to-one task linking
- Retention: quarter + 1 month, saga pattern for batch PUSH
- Suggest algorithms adapted, not rewritten

commit.message:
fix: avoid undefined behavior from unordered_set iterator invalidation in updateExtraFds()

When FileInputStream fds failed to add to epoll (EPERM for regular files),
the code erased from the unordered_set during a range-based for loop, which
is undefined behavior. This caused consistent crashes on aarch64 and
intermittent crashes on x86_64 during HTTP/3 static file serving.
